title: Determinants of age at menarche in the UK: analyses from the Breakthrough Generations Study.
ehr_evidence: Data were collected retrospectively by questionnaire from participants in the Breakthrough Generations Study.
summary: A retrospective analysis of the Breakthrough Generations Study to identify determinants of age at menarche, which is a known risk factor for breast cancer.
key_findings: Early menarche associated with low birthweight, singleton status, prenatal pre-eclampsia, maternal smoking, lack of breastfeeding, non-white ethnicity, heavy/tall stature, and low childhood exercise.
exposure_domain: prenatal and postnatal environmental and biological factors
limitations: Maternal smoking data restricted to first-born children
